tool_args.label: Farebox recovery ratio definition

payload.claim_text: Farebox recovery ratio has a long-term focus. It includes costs that are not funded in the current year, except in an accounting-ledger sense, but are, in effect, passed on to future years; those costs include depreciation and interest on long-term debt. In addition, the recovery ratio allocates centralized MTA services to the Agencies, such as Security, the costs of the Inspector General, Civil Rights, Audit, Risk Management, Legal and Shared Services.
payload.subject: farebox recovery ratio
payload.description: Definition of farebox recovery ratio per the source document note (1)

evidence_refs.source_quote: Row 32: [Farebox Recovery and Operating !A32] (1) Farebox recovery ratio has a long-term focus. It includes costs that are not funded in the current year, except in an accounting-ledger sense, but are, in effect, passed on to future years; those costs include depreciation and interest on long-term debt. In addition, the recovery ratio allocates centralized MTA services to the Agencies, such as Security, the costs of the Inspector General, Civil Rights, Audit, Risk Management, Legal and Shared Services.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Farebox operating ratio definition

payload.claim_text: Farebox operating ratio focuses on Agency operating financial performance. It reflects the way MTA meets its statutory and bond-covenant budget-balancing requirements, and it excludes certain costs that are not subject to Agency control, but are provided centrally by the MTA.
payload.subject: farebox operating ratio
payload.description: Definition of farebox operating ratio per the source document note (2)

evidence_refs.source_quote: Row 33: [Farebox Recovery and Operating !A33] (2) Farebox operating ratio focuses on Agency operating financial performance. It reflects the way MTA meets its statutory and bond-covenant budget-balancing requirements, and it excludes certain costs that are not subject to Agency control, but are provided centrally by the MTA.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Revised methodology note for LIRR and MNR

payload.claim_text: In the agenda materials for the Meeting of the Metro-North and Long Island Rail Road Committees, the calculations of the farebox operating and recovery ratios for the LIRR and MNR use a revised methodology to put the railroads on a more comparable basis. Those statistics, which are included in the respective financial and ridership reports of both Agencies, differ from the statistics presented in this table.
payload.subject: LIRR and MNR farebox ratio methodology
payload.description: Note (3) explaining revised methodology for LIRR and MNR ratios that differ from this table

evidence_refs.source_quote: Row 34: [Farebox Recovery and Operating !A34] (3) In the agenda materials for the Meeting of the Metro-North and Long Island Rail Road Committees, the calculations of the farebox operating and recovery ratios for the LIRR and MNR use a revised methodology to put the railroads on a more comparable basis. Those statistics, which are included in the respective financial and ridership reports of both Agencies, differ from the statistics presented in this table.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: GCMOC expenses included in LIRR ratios

payload.claim_text: Long Island Rail Road farebox operating and recovery ratios include expenses associated with the Grand Central Madison Operating Company (GCMOC), which is responsible for the LIRR-operating portion of Grand Central Terminal.
payload.subject: LIRR farebox ratios
payload.description: Note (4) stating LIRR ratios include GCMOC expenses

evidence_refs.source_quote: Row 35: [Farebox Recovery and Operating !A35] (4) Long Island Rail Road farebox operating and recovery ratios include expenses associated with the Grand Central Madison Operating Company (GCMOC), which is responsible for the LIRR-operating portion of Grand Central Terminal.
